key_departures.impact: Two-year starter and the engine of Denver's top-ranked pass rush interior. Recorded 14.5 sacks over his two Broncos seasons (31 starts). Denver never pursued a re-sign due to projected cost. Interior pass rush loses its most disruptive piece.

Released after one injury-plagued season in Denver. Signed a 3yr/$31.5M deal last March but played only 8 games due to multiple quad injuries, hamstring issues, and a suspension. Saves $6M in cap space with $4.33M dead money. Departure is offset by re-signing Singleton and Strnad.

Broncos declined to tender McLaughlin as a restricted free agent, viewing the minimum $3.52M tender as too high for a depth back. Depth loss only; Dobbins, RJ Harvey, Tyler Badie, and Deuce Vaughan remain.

Started the final four regular-season games and both playoff games in 2025 after Brandon Jones was lost to injury. An unrestricted free agent who is as yet unsigned. Potential hole at safety depth if he departs.

contract.notes: First year $8M fully guaranteed; Year 2 $8M non-guaranteed. Can reach $20M with incentives including $2M for 1,200+ scrimmage yards.

signings.analysis: Dobbins led Denver in rushing (772 yards, 153 carries) in just 10 games before a season-ending foot injury. He is clearly the starter and a clear upgrade over a void at RB1. The $8M AAV is mid-tier for the RB market (Walker got $14M/yr, Etienne $13M/yr) reflecting his injury history. Only Year 1 is fully guaranteed — structure signals a bridge/prove-it component. Denver is banking on health, but likely still wants to draft or sign a complementary back given Dobbins has not played more than 13 games in a season since his rookie year. RB goes from tier-1 need to tier-3 need, but is not closed.

Singleton had 135 tackles in 2025 playing weeks after testicular cancer surgery — an inspiring performance. Returning as the green-dot leader and likely QB-of-the-defense. However, at 32 on a 2-year deal, this is a bridge starter. $7.75M AAV is mid-tier for LB market. Combined with Strnad re-signing, the LB room is stabilized in the short term, but Dre Greenlaw's release means depth is thin. The Broncos are expected to draft a linebacker in April to complement this duo. LB need reduced but not eliminated.

contract.notes: $10M guaranteed; max value $19.5M with $500K/yr playing-time incentives.

signings.analysis: After stepping in for both Singleton (2024 ACL) and Greenlaw (2025 injuries) and starting 8 games each year, Strnad earned a genuine multi-year deal. The 3-year structure at $10M guaranteed is real investment — Denver views him as a co-starter alongside Singleton. Starter-level deal at a below-market AAV; low-risk, high-reward. Still, at 30 on a 3-year deal, the team's LB room still benefits from a younger addition in the draft. This is a depth-stabilizing/co-starter signing, not a top-of-market move.

Trautman is Sean Payton's trusted blocking TE and has started every game for three consecutive seasons in Denver. He is the Y-TE role player, not a receiving threat (20 catches, 195 yards in 2025). His $5.67M AAV is below the mid-tier market for TEs. Securing him retains a critical scheme piece, but the Broncos still lack a true receiving TE option — Evan Engram underperformed in 2025, and the offense craves a weapon at the position. Payton is still expected to pursue a receiving TE via free agency or the draft. TE need unchanged at the receiving/pass-catching layer.

contract.notes: One-year deal; was a restricted free agent kept at below-tender cost.

signings.analysis: Adkins is a versatile depth piece who blocks, catches, lines up at fullback, and contributes on special teams. One-year deal below the RFA tender threshold. Retaining him is housekeeping, not a need-solving move.

Palczewski is one of the NFL's best young swing linemen — started 10 games at LG in 2025 covering for Ben Powers' torn biceps and started at RT in 2024. The 2-year deal with real guarantees shows Denver values him as a potential future starter, especially with Ben Powers' non-guaranteed $12M cap number making him a trade/cut candidate. However, Palczewski is currently depth/swing — IOL is not a need given Powers, McGlinchey, Wattenberg, and Meinerz are all under contract. This is depth protection and upside investment.

QB3 re-sign. Ehlinger reportedly turned down active roster offers from other teams to stay on Denver's practice squad/QB3 role. Both Payton and new OC David Webb have spoken highly of his development. No impact on need picture — Bo Nix and Jarrett Stidham remain QB1/2.

contract.notes: ERFA minimum tender; signed the one-year, $1.075M tender.

signings.analysis: ERFA tender — pure depth retention. Badie is a special teams contributor and emergency RB4 behind Dobbins, RJ Harvey, and Deuce Vaughan.
Special teams ace and safety depth. ERFA tender — roster housekeeping. Does not address any safety starter need.
Depth OLB/EDGE; ERFA tender. Broncos' EDGE room remains intact with Nik Bonitto, Jonathon Cooper, and extended players already in place.

contract.notes: ERFA tender; tender status initially uncertain per reports but ultimately confirmed.

signings.analysis: Interior DL depth retained after Franklin-Myers' departure. Jackson will compete with Sai'vion Jones and Eyioma Uwazurike for snaps vacated by JFM. ERFA tender — does not solve the IDL need, but prevents further erosion of depth.

contract.notes: Second-round RFA tender; pays $5.81M in 2026. Denver placed the tender to retain rights and deter offer sheets.

signings.analysis: McMillian is Denver's starting slot CB and the Broncos view him as a long-term piece — using the higher second-round tender signals they are serious about keeping him. A full extension is expected to follow. However, the tender is only a one-year commitment and CB depth/outside CB2 remain areas to watch. This retains a starter at a key position but does not address any CB2 or outside CB depth needs.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: John Franklin-Myers departed for a 3yr/$63M deal with Tennessee. He recorded 14.5 sacks over two seasons as Denver's most disruptive interior pass rusher. Replacements on roster (Sai'vion Jones, Uwazurike, Jordan Jackson via ERFA tender) are unproven depth. IDL escalates to a top draft priority — early Day 2 at minimum, possibly late first round.

Dobbins re-signed on 2yr/$16M ($8M gtd). He is the clear starter and a top-5 talent when healthy. However, only Year 1 is guaranteed, and he has not played more than 13 games since his 2020 rookie season. RJ Harvey provides depth, but Denver still needs a complementary back. Drops from critical need to watchable need — likely addressed via mid-round draft pick.

Singleton (2yr/$15.5M, $11M gtd) and Strnad (3yr/$18M, $10M gtd) are re-signed as the starting ILB tandem, replacing Greenlaw (released). Both are 30+ years old. Room is stable but aging and thin on depth. Still expect a linebacker drafted in Rounds 1-3 given Sean Payton's emphasis on the position and the 2026 draft's deep LB class.

No WR signings made through Day 1 of legal tampering. Denver has expressed interest in Kendrick Bourne (49ers, 30 years old) but no deal confirmed. WR remains an active need — Bo Nix needs a true WR2 opposite Courtland Sutton. Unchanged; will likely address in free agency or draft.

Trautman re-signed (3yr/$17M) secures the blocking TE role, and Adkins returns as depth. However, the receiving TE need is unchanged — Evan Engram underperformed in 2025, and Payton has made clear he wants an upgrade at the pass-catching TE role. TE remains a priority need, likely addressed via draft or a late FA signing.

P.J. Locke is an unrestricted free agent and as yet unsigned. He filled in as a starter for the final stretch of 2025 and both playoff games. If he departs, Denver's safety depth behind Talanoa Hufanga and Brandon Jones (injury risk) is thin. Devon Key is ERFA depth only. Safety escalates modestly pending Locke's fate.

McMillian retained via 2nd-round RFA tender ($5.81M). Slot CB is secured for 2026. However, this is a one-year commitment — a long-term extension is needed. Outside CB depth remains unchanged. CB stays at tier 3 pending an extension with McMillian.

Palczewski re-signed (2yr/$9.5M) as high-end swing depth. Ben Powers ($18.15M cap hit, non-guaranteed) remains on the roster but is a trade candidate. If Powers is moved, Palczewski becomes the LG starter. IOL is not a pressing need but retains a tier-3 watch given Powers' cap situation.

key_additions.headline: Fan-favorite RB returns after 772-yard, 10-game pace-setter season; only Year 1 guaranteed given injury history
Heart-and-soul linebacker and green-dot leader returns for a fifth Denver season after a 135-tackle 2025 campaign
Emergency starter turned co-starter earns first multi-year deal; solidifies ILB alongside Singleton
Payton's trusted blocking TE anchor locked up for three more years; receiving TE upgrade still needed
Versatile swing lineman who started at LG and RT secured on multi-year deal; potential starter if Ben Powers is traded
Starting slot CB retained via 2nd-round tender; extension expected to follow

key_losses.headline: 14.5-sack interior disruptor over two seasons departs for top-of-market deal; biggest hole to fill this offseason
Marquee 2025 FA signing cut after playing just 8 games due to multiple quad/hamstring injuries and a suspension
Depth RB not tendered as restricted FA; Broncos deemed $3.52M minimum tender too costly for reserve role

notes: All signings through legal tampering period (March 9, 2026). Official league year begins March 11, 2026. No outside free agents signed through Day 1 of legal tampering — Denver focused entirely on retaining own players. Cap space post-FA estimated at ~$18M based on OTC pre-FA figure of $34.7M minus approximately $16-17M in new contract cap hits from Dobbins, Singleton, Strnad, Trautman, Palczewski, Adkins, Ehlinger, and tenders; Greenlaw release adds ~$6M back. Exact post-FA cap figure will be updated when new league year officially opens March 11. Dobbins contract reported as both $16M base/$20M max — base value used as total_value per methodology.
